title: Ericoila prostrata
taxonomy: Ericoila prostrata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Ericoila, which is a small group of plants native to Australia. The Ericoila prostrata is further classified as a low-growing, spreading shrub with small white or pink flowers. The plant is also known as the 'creeping heath' and is native to the eastern and southern regions of Australia. Ericoila prostrata is a popular choice for low-maintenance gardens and landscaping due to its hardy and adaptable nature.

side_effects: Ericoila prostrata, commonly known as trailing heather, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Australia.
